data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/13/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Inch Lower as Investors Assess Oil Prices, Await PCE Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures trended lower early Friday as investors assessed the movement in oil prices amid the escalating Iran war. Also, focus will be on January's personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index reading, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, scheduled for release this morning. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.15%, 0.07%, and 0.04%, respectively, at 5:22 a.m. EST on March 13.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Despite assurances from the Trump administration and the International Energy Agency (IEA) about releasing oil reserves, concerns about oil prices remain as Iran continues to disrupt supply. In fact, Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, said that the crucial Strait of Hormuz will remain shut as a "tool to pressure the enemy." At the time of writing, the price of the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up about 0.45% to $100.91 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) fell 0.11% to trade at $95.62 per barrel. Iran has warned that oil prices could climb to $200 a barrel.  

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major indexes plunged amid a surge in oil prices and escalating geopolitical tension in the Middle East. The Dow Jones fell about 740 points to end the day below the 47,000 mark for the first time this year. Meanwhile, the S&P 500 fell 1.5%, while the Nasdaq Composite declined 1.78%.

Coming to Friday's key economic release, the headline PCE is expected to have gained 0.3% month-over-month and 2.9% year-over-year.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was essentially flat at 4.273%, as of the last check. Also, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price was around $5,088 per ounce.

Elsewhere, European indexes were in the red as investors digested updates on the ongoing U.S.-Iran war and its broader impact.

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Lower on Friday

Asia-Pacific markets closed lower on March 13 amid fears that higher oil prices could trigger a global economic slowdown.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index declined 0.98%. In China, the Shanghai Composite fell 0.82%, while the Shenzhen Component closed 0.65% lower. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei declined 1.16%, with the Topix falling 0.57%.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Micron Stock (MU) Price Target Jumps to $440 Ahead of Q2 Earnings – Should You Buy?
data.description:
Micron Technology (MU) remains on investors' radar as the chipmaker will announce its Q2 FY26 earnings report next week on March 18. Ahead of the announcement, several analysts have raised their price targets, pushing up the average stock forecast for Micron to $440.67, implying an upside of almost 9%. At the same time, the Street-high target of $650 suggests a potential 60% gain. The optimism is driven by strong demand for memory chips and early signs of a rebound in the semiconductor market.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Micron makes NAND chips for long-term storage like SSDs and smartphones, and DRAM chips that act as fast working memory in computers and data centers. Overall, Wall Street expects Micron to post Q2 FY26 earnings of $8.79 per share, marking an impressive 460% jump compared with the same quarter last year. Meanwhile, revenue is forecasted to climb over 135% to $19.13 billion.

Here's what top analysts are saying about Micron stock ahead of its earnings.

Citi Raises MU Price Target on Strong Memory Prices

Earlier this week, Citi's five-star-rated analyst Atif Malik raised his price target for MU stock to $430 from $385, while keeping his Buy rating.

Malik sees potential for Micron (MU) to keep rising, fueled by stronger-than-expected memory prices. Growing demand from data centers and enterprise SSDs is driving DRAM and NAND prices higher. He also pointed out that AI could create a longer memory cycle, similar to the PC boom of the 1990s, which may extend Micron's growth momentum.

Susquehanna Lifts MU Price Target

Likewise, Susquehanna's top analyst, Mehdi Hosseini, raised Micron's price target to $525 from $345 and maintained a Buy rating on the stock. His price target implies an upside of almost 30% from the current level.

Hosseini updated his financial model and raised estimates, pointing to stronger-than-expected pricing in the DRAM and NAND memory markets. He noted that average selling prices for both types of memory are running well above his January forecasts. Moreover, Hosseini expects this momentum to continue into Q2 FY26 and anticipates that FY28 projections will reflect the industry's stronger fundamentals.

Aletheia Boosts MU Price Target to New Street-High of $650

Last week, Aletheia raised its price target on MU to a Street-high of $650 from $315, while maintaining a Buy rating. The firm noted that strong demand for AI training and inference is driving heavy consumption of high-bandwidth memory (HBM). Tight supply across the memory industry further supports a bullish outlook for Micron, as constrained capacity could keep memory prices elevated for longer. Aletheia highlighted that DRAM and NAND production is expected to remain limited through 2026 and 2027, with new NAND cleanrooms unlikely before 2028.

Overall, Aletheia sees Micron growing into one of the world's largest chip suppliers in the coming years. It also expects the company to generate $150–$200 billion in cash flow between FY26 and FY27, giving it considerable financial flexibility.

Is Micron Stock a Good Buy?

Wall Street analysts maintain a Strong Buy rating on MU stock, with 26 Buys and one Hold assigned over the past three months. Micron's average price target of $440.67 implies about 9% upside from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) vs. Micron (MU): Which AI Stock Is the Smarter Buy Before Next Week's Catalysts?
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) and Micron Technology (MU) are both heading into an important week with major catalysts. Nvidia will host its highly anticipated GTC 2026 from March 16–19, where the company is expected to unveil new AI chips, software updates, and potential partnerships. Meanwhile, Micron is set to report its Q2 FY26 earnings on March 18, with analysts expecting record revenue. Using the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool, we compared NVDA and MU to see which stock analysts favor. While both companies carry Strong Buy ratings from analysts, Nvidia offers higher upside potential of almost 50%, compared with a more modest upside of about 9% for Micron.

data.title: Top Wells Fargo Analyst Raises Micron Stock (MU) Price Target Ahead of Q2 Earnings
data.description:
All eyes are on the fiscal second-quarter results of memory and storage solutions provider Micron Technology (MU) on March 18. MU stock has risen 324% over the past year, driven by strong demand for memory chips in the data centers amid the ongoing artificial intelligence (AI) wave and surge in memory prices due to limited supply. Ahead of Q2 FY26 earnings, top Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers reiterated a Buy rating on Micron stock and increased the price target to $470 (about 16% upside potential) from $410.

The solid growth estimates reflect the strong demand for high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and DRAM solutions in data centers.

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Up Today, 3/13/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were modestly higher ahead of Friday's session as investors waited for key U.S. inflation data, the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) report. However, overall sentiment remained cautious as the Iran war continued to weigh on markets despite a slight pullback in oil prices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were up 0.35%, 0.47%, and 0.34%, respectively, at 1:38 a.m. EST on March 13.

Crude prices have eased slightly but remain elevated. At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was modestly lower but still trading above $100 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate fell about 0.53% to around $95.3 per barrel.

Meanwhile, major indices fell sharply on Thursday's regular trading session as oil prices jumped on renewed worries about supply disruptions. The Nasdaq, the Dow, and the S&P 500 dropped 1.8%, 1.6%, and 1.5%, respectively.

The fall came after Iran's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, stated that the Strait of Hormuz should remain closed to pressure enemies.

Coming to the PCE report, consensus expects headline PCE to rise 0.3% month over month and 2.9% from a year ago. Core PCE is projected to increase 0.4% for the month and 3.1% annually.

Further, Q4 GDP (First Revision) will also be released tomorrow. Markets are looking for a revision to the 1.5% growth rate. In addition, the JOLTS Job Openings report on Friday will provide a deeper look into labor demand (job vacancies) for January.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Alibaba (BABA) Jumps into China's New 'AI Craze' with OpenClaw App
data.description:
China's tech giants are racing to cash in on the country's newest AI obsession — OpenClaw, a new type of agentic AI assistant that can carry out real-world tasks for users. The trend has taken off so quickly that students, developers, and even retirees are experimenting with these tools, a phenomenon locals have nicknamed "raising lobsters." Now Alibaba Group (BABA) is jumping into the frenzy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company has launched a new mobile app called "JVS Claw," designed to help users install and run its OpenClaw agentic AI assistant within minutes. The app works on both iOS and Android phones and lets users give simple instructions to AI agents. These agents can help complete tasks such as online shopping, booking travel, or managing basic digital tasks, even for people with no coding skills.

Alibaba is offering the service free for the first 14 days.

China's Tech Firms Race to Build OpenClaw Services

Alibaba's move comes as competition around OpenClaw tools heats up. Baidu (BIDU) released its own Android app earlier this week that uses the assistant to help users shop online or book trips. Other companies, including Tencent (TCEHY) and AI startup Minimax, are also exploring services built around the technology.

The goal is to make agent-based AI easy for everyday users. If widely adopted, these tools could push AI into daily life and create new ways for tech companies to earn money from AI services.

At the same time, authorities are taking a cautious approach. While several local governments have introduced subsidies to support OpenClaw development, Beijing has restricted government agencies and state-owned firms from freely installing these apps on office computers due to data and security risks.

Is Alibaba Stock a Buy Now? 

Wall Street remains constructive on the stock. Alibaba carries a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on eight Buy ratings and one Hold over the past three months. The average BABA price target stands at $197.86, suggesting roughly 45.18% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: ByteDance Plans $2.5B Nvidia AI Buildout Outside China — What It Means for NVDA
data.description:
China's ByteDance, the parent company of TikTok, is reportedly building a large AI computing cluster outside China using Nvidia's (NVDA) newest chips. According to a Wall Street Journal report, ByteDance is working with Southeast Asian cloud firm Aolani Cloud to install about 500 Nvidia Blackwell systems in Malaysia, which could include roughly 36,000 B200 AI chips.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The project could cost more than $2.5 billion. ByteDance plans to use the computing power for AI research and development outside China and to serve growing global demand for its AI services. Aolani said it follows all export control rules and provides cloud services to companies across Asia and other regions.

Why the AI Cluster Is Being Built Outside China

The move comes as Chinese tech companies face tight U.S. limits on advanced AI chips. Building computing power outside China may help ByteDance continue using top hardware while staying within those rules.

Earlier reports said the trump-conditions-cloud-nvidias-ai-chip-sale-to-bytedance">U.S. government was open to allowing ByteDance to buy some Nvidia chips, such as the H200, under strict conditions. However, Nvidia had not agreed to those terms at the time.

What This Means for Nvidia

For Nvidia, the reported project highlights how strong global demand remains for its AI chips. Even with export limits, large tech firms are still spending billions to secure computing power for AI development.

If confirmed, the Malaysia project would show how companies are building AI infrastructure in new regions to access Nvidia's latest chips and keep pace in the fast-growing AI race.

Is Nvidia a Buy or Sell Stock?

According to TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 38 Buys and one Hold assigned in the last three months. At $273.61, the Nvidia average share price target implies a 49.40% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Adobe Stock Plunges on CEO Exit and AI Restructuring Fears
data.description:
Adobe stock (ADBE) dropped sharply today after the company announced that its longtime leader, Shantanu Narayen, plans to step down. ADBE stock fell over 1% during regular trading and continued to slide in after-hours trading, hitting lows near $248. Investors are nervous because Narayen has led the company for nearly 20 years, and his exit comes right as Adobe faces a massive challenge from new AI tools that could change its business forever.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Narayen Steps Down at a Major Turning Point

Shantanu Narayen has been the face of Adobe since 2007. Under his leadership, the company grew from a small software firm into a giant worth billions. However, Adobe announced on Thursday that he will leave his role as CEO but stay on as board chair. In a letter to his team, Narayen wrote that "The next era of creativity is being written right now—shaped by AI." The company is now searching for a new leader who can handle this fast-moving shift toward automation and smart design tools.

AI Forces Big Tech to Shrink Teams

The news at Adobe is part of a much bigger trend across the tech world. Many companies are cutting staff as they move toward using AI to do more work with fewer people. For example, Atlassian (TEAM) just cut 1,600 jobs to focus more on AI, and Jack Dorsey's company, Block (XYZ), cut over 4,000 people for the same reason. Experts told Decrypt that tech firms are "pivoting their operations around the AI narrative" to stay efficient. This means demand for AI experts is soaring, while older, routine jobs are being eliminated.

The "Uncomfortable Truth" about Automation

Industry analysts are calling this a period of "rapid realignment." As AI tools become better at coding and design, companies no longer need huge teams of junior employees. According to Decrypt, one analyst described the situation as an "uncomfortable truth," if a worker uses AI to automate their own tasks; they might be proving that their role is no longer needed. So far, trackers estimate that over 76,000 jobs have been lost globally due to AI-related changes, with most of those cuts happening in the U.S.

In addition, investors are worried that Adobe's core products, like Photoshop, are being challenged by new AI platforms that can create images and videos from simple text prompts. By changing leadership now, Adobe is trying to show it is ready to fight back. However, the stock's big drop shows that the market isn't convinced yet.

Is Adobe Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on ADBE stock based on six Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average ADBE price target of $370.24 per share implies 37.2% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market Today Review: SPY, QQQ Tumble as Oil Approaches $100 on Iranian Leader's Vow to Keep Hormuz Closed
data.description:
It was another rough day for the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ), as both ETFs closed down by over 1.5% on Thursday as the U.S.-Iran war dragged on into its 13th day.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

News That Moved the Stock Market Today

  • In his first publicly broadcast message as Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei said that the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed as a "tool to pressure the enemy" and vowed to strike U.S. bases in the Middle East. Crude oil futures (CL) surged 10% to $95 following his comments.
  • The Trump administration announced trade investigations into 16 countries, including China, the European Union, Mexico, and India, under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974. The investigation centers on excess capacity and production and could lead to new tariffs and fines.
  • U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer noted that the U.S. would announce additional Section 301 trade investigations against 60 countries as soon as Thursday.
  • The U.S. trade deficit fell by 25.3% to $54.5 billion as exports jumped to a record high of $302.1 billion.
  • Microsoft (MSFT) announced a partnership with AMD (AMD) to develop its next-generation Xbox console.
  • Morgan Stanley (MS) closed in the red after the firm joined a growing number of financial firms to limit withdrawals from one of its private credit funds.
  • Palantir (PLTR) finished higher after announcing new collaborations with Nvidia (NVDA), GE Aerospace (GE), and Ondas (ONDS) at its AIPCon 9 event.

Today's Best-Performing Sector 

Energy was the best-performing sector for a second consecutive day amid rising oil prices and the risk of a prolonged closure to the Strait of Hormuz. Higher oil prices typically boost profits for oil producers, driving gains in stocks like Occidental Petroleum (OXY) and Chevron (CVX) as investors anticipate stronger earnings.

Several energy stocks led the sector's gains, including:

  • Occidental Petroleum (OXY)
  • Phillips 66 (PSX)
  • Chevron (CVX)
  • Marathon Petroleum (MPC)
  • Valero Energy (VLO)

Today's Worst-Performing Sector 

Meanwhile, industrial stocks posted the worst performance on Thursday. With higher gas prices, transportation and travel companies face increased operating costs, weighing on profits and margins. The sector also struggled as investors assessed the impact of supply chain challenges and global trade uncertainty.

Notable industrial stocks trading lower include:

  • Southwest Airlines (LUV)
  • Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL)
  • GE Aerospace (GE)
  • United Rentals (URI)
  • Emerson Electric (EMR)
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: BlackRock's New Staked Ethereum ETF (ETHB) Sees 'Very, Very Solid' $15.5 Million Debut, Says Analyst
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) has officially launched its newest crypto fund, the iShares Staked Ethereum Trust (ETHB), and it is already off to a strong start. On its first day of trading this Thursday, the fund saw about $15.5 million in total volume. While this was less than some Solana (SOL-USD) funds that launched last year, experts etfs-hold-steady">say the debut was very successful. This new fund is unique because it doesn't just hold Ethereum (ETH-USD); it also "stakes" it to earn extra rewards for investors.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

BlackRock's ETF Offers a Yield

The big draw for this new ETF is that it offers a way to earn a yield, which is usually around 4% a year. The fund keeps 80% of its Ethereum in staking, where the digital coins are put to work to help run the network. In return, the network pays out rewards. BlackRock has teamed up with professional companies like Galaxy Digital (GLXY) and Figment to handle this work. Best of all, these rewards are sent out to investors every month, making it feel more like a traditional dividend payment.

It's a Low-Cost Way for Investors to Join the Market

To get more people to use the fund, BlackRock is offering a special deal on fees. The normal cost to own the fund is 0.25%, but they are cutting that in half to 0.12% for the first year. This discount applies to the first $2.5 billion that people invest. This cheaper price point helps BlackRock compete with other funds. The company hopes to repeat the success of its Bitcoin fund (IBIT), which has already brought in over $62 billion since it started.

How It Compares to Other Big Launches

Even though $15.5 million is a great result for a first day, it still trails behind some other popular crypto funds. For example, a Solana staking fund from Bitwise (BSOL) saw over $55 million on its first day last October. However, Bloomberg analyst James Seyffart still called the BlackRock launch "very, very solid." It shows that big institutional investors are still very interested in Ethereum, especially when they can earn extra rewards on top of the price going up.

Is BlackRock a Good Stock to Buy?

According to data from two Wall Street analysts over the past three months, BLK stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys and one Hold rating.

The average 12-month BLK price target stands at $1,350.30, implying a potential upside of 46.3% from the current price.
data.author: Annika Masrani
